item.title: Meta Eyes AI Subscriptions While AI Rivals Target Meta’s Ad Business
item.description: The social giant wants to make money from its chatbot, but its core business may be at risk.

item.title: South Korea Grapples With an AI Boom Spurring $340,000 Bonuses
item.description: Policymakers in South Korea are grappling with how to manage the AI-fueled chip boom.

item.title: Mexico’s Record Exports to the US Show an Underlying Shift
item.description: Trump’s trade threats are stirring uncertainty over USMCA negotiations this year

item.title: After Rich Colleges Raid Basketball Team, School Exits D1 Sports
item.description: Saint Francis University was out of its league, and Father Malachi Van Tassell knew it.

item.title: Universal’s First Kid-Focused Theme Park to Open in Texas on July 1
item.description: Comcast Corp.’s Universal division will open its first theme park aimed specifically at young children this summer in an effort to reach a demographic that may be overwhelmed by some of the attractions at its bigger parks.

item.title: UK Could Miss Housing Target by 40% or More, Research Finds
item.description: The UK could miss its goal of adding 1.5 million new homes in the current parliamentary term by at least 40%, according to a report by Bloomberg Intelligence that urges the government to ramp up efforts to stimulate demand and help developers.

item.title: The AI Boom Is Warping the San Francisco Property Market
item.description: Ordinary renters are getting squeezed out.

item.title: Micron’s Rally to $1 Trillion Has Actual Earnings Behind It
item.description: Still, investors are skeptical those earnings are durable.

item.title: Senegal’s Looming Bond Payments Are Next Test Ahead of IMF Talks
item.description: Senegal has two interest payments on foreign-currency bonds due next month and while analysts expect them to proceed, they come at a tense political time in the West African nation ahead of talks with the International Monetary Fund.

item.title: Retail Revival Adds Fuel to US Stocks, JPMorgan Strategists Say
item.description: Retail investors’ share of US equity trading is poised to rebound after falling to a four-year low by the end of the first quarter, according to JPMorgan Chase & Co. strategists, potentially providing a fresh tailwind for stocks.

item.title: Temu Fined €200 Million by EU Over Dangerous Toys, Chargers
item.description: Temu, the Chinese e-commerce giant, was fined €200 million ($232 million) by the European Union for failing to stop sales of unsafe baby toys and chargers on the platform.

item.title: Warsh’s New Challenge: Containing Fed Hawks as Inflation Simmers
item.description: Kevin Warsh won the race for Federal Reserve chairman partly by mapping out a route to lower interest rates. Now, the new Fed chief faces a starkly different dynamic: How to restrain a sudden shift in expectations for higher rates as fellow policymakers warn that inflation is back.

item.title: Drone Makers Quantum and Destinus Seek Banks for 2027 IPOs
item.description: Quantum Systems and Destinus are in talks to line up advisers for potential IPOs as soon as early 2027, people familiar with the matter said, as the drone makers seek to tap surging demand for unmanned defense assets.

item.title: JD.com’s €2.2 Billion Ceconomy Deal Hit by EU Subsidy Probe
item.description: Chinese e-commerce firm JD.com Inc.’s offer to acquire Germany’s Ceconomy AG has been hit by an in-depth probe by European Union watchdogs, throwing the €2.2 billion ($2.6 billion) deal for the bloc’s largest consumer electronics retailer into doubt.

item.title: The DOJ Wants to Know Who on Reddit and X Is Criticizing ICE's Tactics
item.description: In seeking identities of those behind anonymous social media posts, the Trump administration is intensifying its pursuit using grand jury subpoenas.

item.title: Hedge Funds Control More Than Half of Electronic Gilts Trading
item.description: Hedge funds now control more than half of the electronically traded gilts market, Tradeweb data show, a shift that could be making UK government debt more vulnerable to bursts of volatility.

item.title: Emerging Stock Rally Stalls After Setback to Iran Accord Hopes
item.description: Emerging-market stocks snapped a week-long rally and most currencies weakened as renewed strikes on Iran dashed hopes of a swift resolution to the war.

item.title: Micron’s Cheap Stock Valuation Seen as a Contrarian Warning Sign
item.description: Micron Technology Inc. is the third-best performer in the S&P 500 Index this year and at the same time one of the least expensive stocks in the market. While that dichotomy looks bullish on the surface, it may be more of a warning sign than a green light for investors.

item.title: Korea’s Pension Fund Raises Domestic Stock Target as Kospi Soars
item.description: South Korea’s giant pension fund sharply ramped up its allocation target for domestic equities, a move that may help it avoid forced sales of Kospi shares after the recent rally pushed its holdings above the limit.

item.title: Russia Plans to Launch Crewed Spacecraft From Baikonur in July
item.description: Russia plans its first launch of a manned space mission from the Baikonur launch site this year with a crew that includes a NASA astronaut.

item.title: Deutsche Bank Chairman Pay Hike Rejected as Too Much by Investor
item.description: Deutsche Bank AG’s plan to give a big pay raise to Supervisory Board Chairman Alexander Wynaendts drew criticism at the lender’s annual general meeting.

item.title: Guangzhou Exchange Said to Study Night Trading for Platinum
item.description: China’s Guangzhou Futures Exchange is exploring launching night trading, primarily for platinum and palladium contracts, in response to trader feedback, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: What It Takes to Get a Job at Anthropic
item.description: To win a coveted role, candidates shouldn’t outsource their thinking to AI — and should be prepared to talk about their worldview.

item.title: Venezuelans Back Dollarization to Rein in 600% Inflation Rate
item.description: More than half of Venezuelans support dollarizing the economy to rein in one of the world’s fastest inflation rates.

item.title: Mistral Signs Airbus and BMW as it Brings AI to Manufacturing
item.description: French artificial intelligence startup Mistral AI is expanding into advanced manufacturing, striking deals with new customers Airbus SE and BMW AG as it looks to so-called physical AI to fuel growth.

item.title: What Is Joint Debt and Why Is Europe Talking About It Again?
item.description: Economic pressure from the US and China is forcing some European nations to confront their historic aversion to joint borrowing.

item.title: AtlasEdge Gets €1.2 Billion in Loans for European Data Centers
item.description: A data center operator backed by Liberty Global and DigitalBridge Group Inc. secured more than €1.2 billion ($1.4 billion) in financing to roll out artificial intelligence infrastructure in under-served parts of Europe.

item.title: Zambia Defies Iran War Shock With Inflation Still Cooling
item.description: Zambia’s annual inflation slowed to a more than eight-year low in May as a stronger kwacha and a bumper corn harvest tamed prices.

item.title: Russia Starts Offering Yuan Bonds After Putin’s Visit to Beijing
item.description: Russia started accepting bids for its second sale of yuan-denominated sovereign bonds after President Vladimir Putin held talks in Beijing last week.

item.title: French Power Demand Rises as Deadly Heat Wave Intensifies
item.description: The deadly heat wave gripping northwest Europe is set to intensify in Spain and France, lifting power demand for cooling and threatening fresh temperature records.
